items.promotion_gate: Open this lane only when LONG_SPAN_BUNDLE_GATE reports every prerequisite gate met or an intentional override is documented with new evidence.

items.action: Interpret the current bounded planted-density scale sweeps before extending size.
items.why_now: The current 16 MiB planted-density sweep is useful performance evidence but not natural-corpus proof; it now preserves seed expansions, target windows, lookup counts, and target-table byte estimates with peak/estimated-table ratio 21.98. SCALE_PERFORMANCE reports plateau ratio spread 6.514%, largest peak memory 1934.602 MiB, and next-double peak estimate 3869.204 MiB. BOUNDED_STREAMING_MEMORY_GATE reports chunked_target_table_fixture_evidence_no_rss, target-table preflight present True, full RSS containment False, and chunked target tables True.
items.promotion_gate: Peak memory and runtime scale predictably without changing roundtrip or telemetry correctness, with full RSS containment or chunked target tables for promoted workloads.
items.stop_rule: Stop scaling if peak working set grows faster than search-work and target-table telemetry explains, or if target-table estimates exceed the configured memory limit.
items.suggested_artifact: Use `docs/SCALE_PERFORMANCE.md` and `docs/BOUNDED_STREAMING_MEMORY_GATE.md`; extend bounded sizes only if the current ratio stays explainable or chunked tables reduce memory.
